messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and file structure",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize npm, create src and public directories, and add a README and .gitignore.",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A git repo (or folder) contains package.json, src/ and public/ directories, README.md, and .gitignore; npm init completed without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install backend and tooling dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and development tools (nodemon or pm2) plus build tools for Tailwind (postcss, autoprefixer, tailwindcss) and add relevant npm scripts.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "package.json lists express, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer (and dev tools) and npm install finishes with no errors; scripts for dev and build added."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build and input stylesheet",
      "description": "Generate tailwind.config.js, configure PostCSS if needed, create a Tailwind input CSS file (e.g., src/styles/tailwind.css) and add a build script that outputs to public/css.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js exists, input CSS contains Tailwind directives, and running the build script produces a public/css/main.css with Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server-with-api",
      "title": "Create Express server and color API",
      "description": "Implement an Express server that serves static files from public/, and exposes a simple REST API (GET /api/color, POST /api/color) that stores the current text color in memory.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies",
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express app runs, serves public/index.html, and GET/POST /api/color return and accept JSON { color: \"#rrggbb\" } with in-memory persistence for the running process."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Create frontend UI with Tailwind styling",
      "description": "Create public/index.html that uses the built Tailwind CSS and contains the 'hello world' text element and UI controls (color input or palette) to choose colors visually.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "public/index.html renders with Tailwind styles, displays 'hello world' text, and includes accessible UI controls to pick a color before integration with the API."
    },
    {
      "id": "connect-frontend-to-api",
      "title": "Wire frontend controls to the Express API",
      "description": "Add client-side JavaScript that calls GET /api/color on load to set the text color and POST /api/color when the user selects a new color; update the displayed text immediately.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-ui",
        "create-express-server-with-api"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "On page load the client fetches current color and applies it; when user changes color the client posts to /api/color, server saves it, and the UI updates without reload."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test flows and add developer documentation",
      "description": "Manually test UI and API flows, fix issues, and update README with run/build instructions, example requests, and how to change styling or persist color.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "connect-frontend-to-api"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All primary flows tested (serve app, change color, reload preserves color for runtime), README includes setup and run steps, and any discovered bugs are logged/fixed."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: set up project and dependencies first, configure styling build, implement backend server and API, build frontend UI, then integrate frontend with API and finish with testing and documentation."
}
